title: ExecutionResult
description: Discriminated result of an execution attempt. Protocol version 1.0.0. The 'mode' field discriminates between BrokeredExecutionResult and ResourceOwnedExecutionResult. Each mode has its own state machine; the states MUST NOT be mixed. See protocol/03-execution-modes.md and protocol/12-execution-results.md.

brokered_execution_state.description: Terminal state of a brokered execution. 'succeeded' = provider execution was observed and returned a success response. 'failed' = provider execution was observed and returned a failure. 'refused' = the broker refused to execute (proof invalid, action out of scope, credential missing, etc.) - no provider call was attempted. 'outcome_unknown' = the provider call was attempted but the outcome could not be determined (timeout, partial response, reconciliation pending).

resource_owned_execution_state.description: Terminal state of a resource-owned execution. 'submitted' = the request+proof were submitted to the resource boundary but no ack has been received. 'accepted' = the resource accepted the request but has not yet completed execution. 'refused' = the resource refused the request (proof invalid, action out of scope, resource policy denied). 'succeeded' = the resource executed the action and returned a verifiable receipt. 'failed' = the resource executed the action and it failed. 'outcome_unknown' = the resource returned no receipt or an unverifiable receipt; the final state cannot be determined. 'submitted' and 'accepted' are NOT success states - submission is not execution.

finality_status.description: Whether the result represents a final outcome. 'final' = no further state transitions are expected; the outcome is authoritative. 'non_final' = the outcome may change as reconciliation or resource receipts arrive. 'succeeded', 'failed', and 'refused' are final in both modes. 'outcome_unknown' is non_final. 'submitted' and 'accepted' (resource_owned only) are non_final.

verified_by.description: Identifier of the component that verified the proof. Example: 'actenon-permit-broker', 'actenon-kernel-verifier'.

executed_by.description: Identifier of the component that invoked the provider. In brokered mode this is the broker. Example: 'actenon-permit-broker'.

provider_execution_observed.description: True if the broker observed the provider's response (success or failure). False if the call timed out, returned a partial response, or never returned. 'succeeded' and 'failed' require this to be true.

receipt_received.description: True if a receipt was issued for this execution. Brokered mode always issues a receipt on observed outcomes.

receipt_verified.description: True if the receipt was cryptographically verified. False if no receipt was issued or verification failed.

attempt_id.description: The execution attempt id, matching the receipt's execution_attempt_id. MUST use the 'exec_' prefix.

provider_evidence.description: Redacted evidence from the provider response. MUST NOT contain credentials.

verified_by.description: Identifier of the component that verified the proof at the resource boundary. Example: 'resource-boundary-stripe', 'iam-control-plane-acme'.

executed_by.description: Identifier of the resource that executed the action. Example: 'stripe', 'acme-iam'.

provider_execution_observed.description: True if the resource reported an execution outcome (success or failure). False for 'submitted' and 'accepted' states - submission is not observation.

resource_receipt_received.description: True if the resource returned a receipt. False for 'submitted' and 'accepted'. 'succeeded' requires this to be true.

resource_receipt_verified.description: True if the resource receipt's signature was cryptographically verified against the resource's published signing key. False if no receipt was received or signature verification failed.

resource_receipt.description: The resource's receipt payload, if received. MUST be cryptographically verified before being trusted.

submission_reference.description: Reference returned by the resource boundary on submission. Used to correlate later resource receipts.
